ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists specifically for a chicken and mushroom melt sandwich. FOODON_00002473 (sandwich food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Sandwich) is the closest parent. No specific chicken-mushroom melt sub-entry exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559009 (Sandwich) is the closest available concept. No specific chicken mushroom melt sandwich concept exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard ala carte serving is approximately one 6-inch toasted sub roll (approximately 230 g) filled with chicken strips, sautéed mushrooms, melted cheese, and salad vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken & Bacon Ranch Melt / Chicken sub composite — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Subway nutritional data adapted for chicken-mushroom-cheese sub
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy) — adapted estimate
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for a Chicken and Mushroom Melt sandwich as served at Subway Singapore. Values are estimated by component-based calculation using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for: cooked chicken breast (FDC ID 171477), white mushrooms cooked (FDC ID 169251), processed cheese (FDC ID 173414), wheat sub roll (FDC ID 174924), and standard salad vegetables. Proportions are based on a typical 6-inch sub composition (approximately 230 g total). Values are expressed per 100 g of the assembled sandwich.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of bread and sandwich products — analogous estimate based on wheat sub roll GI and mixed sandwich composition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a chicken and mushroom melt sub. GI of 53 is estimated based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white wheat bread / sub roll (GI approximately 55) adjusted downward to approximately 53 to account for the protein and fat content of the chicken, cheese, and mushroom filling, which slow gastric emptying and reduce the glycemic response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (53 × 55.2) / 100 ≈ 29.3, rounded to 29.6 using 24.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 230 g serving = 55.2 g carbohydrate per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (approximately 520 mg per 100 g; approximately 1196 mg per 230 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this sandwich due to its high sodium content, which can contribute significantly to the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; requesting reduced-sodium sauces and no added salt can partially mitigate the risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (GL approximately 29.6 per serving) driven by the wheat sub roll
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that the wheat sub roll contributes a moderate glycemic load per serving; choosing a wholegrain or lower-GI bread option, reducing portion size, and pairing with non-starchy vegetables can help moderate the postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (approximately 3.2 g per 100 g) and high sodium from cheese and sauces
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the saturated fat from melted cheese and the high sodium content are the primary concerns; limiting portion frequency, requesting low-fat cheese, and avoiding high-fat sauces such as mayonnaise can reduce cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Chicken and Mushroom Melt Sandwich is a toasted Western-style sub sandwich consisting of seasoned chicken breast strips, sautéed or marinated mushrooms, and melted cheese (typically mozzarella or processed cheese) layered inside a toasted wheat sub roll and topped with fresh salad vegetables such as lettuce, tomato, onion, and capsicum, with optional sauces such as mayonnaise or chipotle. It is commonly served as an ala carte item at fast-casual sandwich chains such as Subway in Singapore. The sandwich has a warm, savoury flavour profile with umami notes from the mushrooms and a creamy, slightly salty finish from the melted cheese. The texture combines the soft chew of the toasted bread, the tender chicken, and the slight bite of fresh vegetables. Nutritionally, it provides a moderate energy density of approximately 218 kcal per 100 g, with a meaningful protein contribution of approximately 13.5 g per 100 g from the chicken and cheese, making it a relatively protein-rich fast-casual meal option. The primary nutritional concern is its sodium content, estimated at approximately 520 mg per 100 g, which is elevated due to the cheese, sauces, and seasoned chicken. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 53 (low GI range), though the glycemic load per full serving is moderate at approximately 29.6 due to the carbohydrate contribution of the sub roll. This sandwich is suitable for halal diets and contains common allergens including gluten, dairy, and egg (from mayonnaise). It is not suitable for vegetarian or vegan diets.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for a Chicken and Mushroom Melt sandwich exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated by component-based calculation using USDA SR Legacy entries for individual ingredients (cooked chicken breast, cooked mushrooms, processed cheese, wheat sub roll, salad vegetables) in proportions consistent with a standard 6-inch sub. GI is an analogous estimate anchored to the University of Sydney GI Database wheat bread entry. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-based estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries; no exact composite entry for this specific sandwich exists in any approved primary source. 2. Sodium estimate (520 mg per 100 g) reflects typical fast-casual sandwich sodium levels including cheese and sauce contributions; actual sodium may vary by restaurant preparation and sauce selection. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported across the component USDA SR Legacy entries used for this estimate. 4. vitamin_e_mg is set to -1 as it is not consistently reported in the component USDA SR Legacy entries used for this estimate. 5. selenium_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the component USDA SR Legacy entries used for this estimate. 6. GI of 53 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database wheat bread/sub roll entry, adjusted for the protein and fat content of the filling; gi_category is low. 7. Halal status is assumed based on Subway Singapore's halal-certified operations; individual restaurant halal status should be verified. 8. The input food_group was listed as 'Vegetables' which is incorrect for this mixed sandwich product; corrected to 'Mixed'. 9. Serving size of 230 g is based on a standard 6-inch Subway sub assembled weight.
